query: Treasury Department response and intervention
reference_summary: Henry Paulson speaks during a news conference in Washington .Henry Paulson says the government will start buying securities backed by mortgages -- initially , up to $ 5 billion worth .Henry Paulson , announces a government plan to remedy the crisis .Treasury Dept. offers to protect investments in money market funds .Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac will increase their purchases of mortgage-backed securities , Treasury Dept. says .Fearing massive transfers from bank accounts to money market funds , the Treasury Dept. says it will only guarantee existing investments in money market funds .Gov. Arnold Schwarzenegger sends a letter to Treas. Sec .Henry Paulson warning that the state of California is running low on cash and may need an emergency $ 7 billion loan within weeks .The Treasury Dept. says on Sunday that it will tap Neel Kashkari , an assistant secretary of international affairs and a former Goldman Sachs banker , to oversee the government 's $ 700 billion financial rescue program .The Federal Reserve takes steps to funnel more money into the banking system while the U.S. Treasury says it will increase its bond sales to pay for the $ 700 billion rescue plan .Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson says global markets remain strained .-LRB- AP video -RRB- Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson urges patience as global markets continue to slide .The Treasury Dept. says is would allow the government to buy stakes in banks .Treasury Secretary Paulson lays out steps to shore up the nation 's banking system by having the government buy shares in leading banks .Fed Chairman Ben Bernanke and Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson discuss the federal plan to shore up the nation 's banking system .Treasury Dept. hires the law firm of Simpson , Thacher and Bartlett for six months at a rate of $ 300,000 to be the legal advisers on the $ 700 billion Wall Street bailout .Henry Paulson 's Treasury Department said its investments in banks would count as `` Tier 1 '' capital .Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson holds a news conference at the Treasury Department .Treasury announces that it has hired PricewaterhouseCoopers and Ernst & Young to be the accountants for the $ 700 billion Wall Street bailout plan .Treasury hires James H. Lambright to be the chief investment officer of the $ 700 billion Wall Street bailout\/rescue plan .Treasury Dept. says it will announce that a group of large regional banks have agreed to accept investments from the government , joining nine of the largest American banks who were forced to accept $ 125 billion in funding last week .-LRB- Seth Perlman \/ AP -RRB- The Treasury Dept. says it is working on ways to broaden its $ 700 billion bank rescue program to help insurance companies that are a critical backstop to a wide range of deals .The White House says that financing companies owned by Detroit automakers could qualify for assistance under the $ 700 billion financial bailout package .The Treasury and FDIC say they are crafting a plan under which the government would guarantee the mortgages of as many as 3 million homeowners now struggling to avoid foreclosure .The Post reports that U.S. banks getting more than $ 163 billion from the Treasury Department for new lending will pay more than half of that sum to their shareholders , with government permission , over the next three years .Neel Kashkari , Treasury Depart .assistant secretary for financial stability , left , and Federal Housing Finance Agency director James B. Lockhart III , announce a new program to help at-risk homeowners .Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson says that the $ 700 billion government rescue program will not be used to purchase troubled assets as originally planned .-LRB- AP video -RRB- Treas. Sec Henry Paulson says the $ 700 billion government rescue program will not be used to purchase troubled assets as originally planned and that the main priority of the bailout program would be to bolster banks and consumer lenders .Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson , Federal Reserve Chairman Ben Bernanke and Federal Deposit Insurance Corp. -LRB- FDIC -RRB- Chairwoman Sheila Bair testify on Capitol Hill .In a hearing with the House Financial Services Committee , Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson defends his decisions on how to use the $ 700 billion financial rescue package .The Treasury extends until April 30 , 2009 , its guarantee on deposits in money-market funds , which was put into place on Sept. 19 .The government introduced a pair of new programs Tuesday that will provide $ 800 billion to help unfreeze the market for consumer debt which Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson calls vital to supporting the economy .-LRB- AP Video -RRB- The Federal Reserve and Treasury move to boost consumer spending and lower home mortgage rates by committing up to $ 800 billion to make it easier for households to borrow money for cars , tuition bills and new homes .Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson gives an update on the federal bailout while speaking at the Fortune 500 Forum in Washington -LRB- Gerald Herbert - AP -RRB- Economists with the nonprofit National Bureau of Economic Research announce that the U.S. economy entered a recession in December 2007 .Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson says that the administration is looking for more ways to tap a $ 700 billion financial rescue program. The Treasury Department considers a plan to intervene directly in the mortgage industry to dramatically force down rates and stimulate the housing market .

query: Ben Bernanke and Federal Reserve Monetary response
reference_summary: The Dow Jones industrial average rebounds on the news that the Fed has agreed to help AIG .Global Lenders The Fed and central banks in Europe , Japan and Canada team up to inject as much as $ 180 billion into global markets to ease the cash crunch , while investors withdraw $ 80 billion from money market funds .President Bush , with Federal Reserve chairman Ben Bernanke and Treas. Sec .During his second day of testimony on Capitol Hill , Federal Reserve chairman Ben Bernanke tells lawmakers the U.S. economy is faltering .The Federal Reserve injects hundreds of billions of dollars into the world financial system in response to a plunge in overseas financial markets as a wave of new bank troubles hit Europe .The Federal Reserve takes steps to funnel more money into the banking system while the U.S. Treasury says it will increase its bond sales to pay for the $ 700 billion rescue plan .Fed chairman Ben Bernanke says there is more economic pain ahead .'The Federal Reserve says it will buy up the short-term debt that funds the daily operations of banks and ordinary businesses .Federal Reserve chairman Ben Bernanke says he is open to cutting interest rates at the Fed 's next meeting on Oct. 28 .The Federal Reserve and European Central banks simultaneously order emergency rate cuts of a half a percentage point early in the morning .European governments put hundreds of billions of dollars into their banking systems and the U.S. Federal Reserve says it will back up their effort by making U.S. currency available in unlimited amounts .Fed Chairman Ben Bernanke and Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson discuss the federal plan to shore up the nation 's banking system .Federal Reserve Chairman Ben Bernanke tells Congress a fresh round of government stimulus is a good idea .-LRB- AP Photo\/Gerald Herbert -RRB- The Fed says it will make up to $ 540 billion available to buy assets from money-market mutual funds in order to keep the funds from experiencing cash crunches .-LRB- David Zalubowski \/ AP -RRB- The Federal Reserve begins buying short-term debt from banks and companies as a way to make it easier for corporate America to borrow cash to cover day-to-day operations .The Federal Reserve cuts the key Federal Funds rate by half a point to one percent , matching its lowest level in decades .The Federal Reserve says it will lend money for the first time to central banks in several emerging nations , in order to prevent a global shortage of dollars .-LRB- Manuel Balce Ceneta \/ AP -RRB- In a speech to a forum on housing finance , Federal Reserve Chairman Ben Bernanke endorses some government backing of home loans .Federal Reserve Chairman Ben Bernanke delivers his speech at the fifth ECB Central Banking Conference in Frankfurt , Germany .Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson , Federal Reserve Chairman Ben Bernanke and Federal Deposit Insurance Corp. -LRB- FDIC -RRB- Chairwoman Sheila Bair testify on Capitol Hill .Federal Reserve leaders release projections indicating they expect the economy to worsen significantly in the coming year and the joblessness rate to rise to 8 percent at the end of 2009 .-LRB- AP Video -RRB- The Federal Reserve and Treasury move to boost consumer spending and lower home mortgage rates by committing up to $ 800 billion to make it easier for households to borrow money for cars , tuition bills and new homes .'Federal Reserve Chairman Ben Bernanke says that he is inclined to keep cutting interest rates to try to contain damage from the downturn .Reports in the Fed 's Beige Book show business conditions weakened across the United States as private employers shed jobs and the nation 's service sector contracted .A new Fed report shows that Americans have cut back on their debt levels for the first time on record while falling home values and tightening credit standards drove down the net worth of American households by nearly 5 percent .

query: The impact and response of car companies
reference_summary: Auto sales plummet 27 % in September on a year-over-year basis as credit tightens .General Motors , Ford , Toyota and Chrysler all report double-digit sales drops .General Motors shares fall 31 percent to $ 4.76 after J.D. Power says the auto industry might collapse in 2009 .GM issues a statement saying bankruptcy is `` not an option . September retail sales fell by the largest amount in three years as Americans stayed away from malls and nixed plans for new cars .General Motors suspends a variety of benefit programs for its white-collar workers and slashes additional jobs to cut costs .Chrysler says it will cut 25 percent of its white collar workforce by the end of the year .The White House says that financing companies owned by Detroit automakers could qualify for assistance under the $ 700 billion financial bailout package .GMAC , the financing arm of General Motors , says it is in negotiations to become a bank holding company , a shift that would allow it to take advantage of the government 's $ 700 billion financial rescue package .Ford 's sales dropped 30 percent in October .-LRB- AP Photo\/David Zalubowski -RRB- U.S. automakers announce huge drops in third quarter sales figures .The Center for Automotive Research says that if Detroit automakers GM , Ford and Chrysler fail , 3 million jobs across the entire auto sector will be lost in the first year of collapse .Ford and General Motors report dismal third quarter results , adding urgency to the request by the Big Three automakers for an additional $ 25 billion in federal assistance .House Speaker Nancy Pelosi says the House will convene next week to vote on a plan to provide emergency cash to the nation 's battered automobile industry .Maryland Senator Barbara Mikulski unveils a proposal to allow car buyers to deduct the interest on car loans and sales tax from their income taxes as a way to help the ailing U.S. auto industry .-LRB- AP Photo\/Evan Vucci -RRB- The CEO 's of the big three Detroit automakers , GM , Ford and Chrysler , plead for assistance on Capitol Hill .Chrysler CEO Robert Nardelli , right , and GM chief Richard Wagoner told Congress that they urgently need federal aid .The executives of the `` Big Three '' automakers appear on Capitol Hill to plead for $ 25 billion in federal aid .Democrats reject the proposed auto bailout deal .-LRB- AP Photo\/Gerald Herbert -RRB- Stocks plummet for the second day in a row as prospects for a bailout of the U.S. auto industry dim .Lawmakers fail to reach agreement on a proposal to provide $ 25 billion in emergency aid to the ailing auto industry , but say they may come back after Thanksgiving to try again .General Motors 's financial arm , GMAC Financial Services , applies to become a bank holding company , a move that could allow it to grab a piece of the Treasury Department 's $ 700 billion rescue package .Car manufacturer General Motors says it will briefly idle five manufacturing plants over the next two months to cut costs .House Speaker Nancy Pelosi -LRB- D-Calif . -RRB-and Senate Leader Harry Reid -LRB- D-Nev . -RRB- send a letter to the heads of GM , Ford and Chrysler spelling out the terms that the troubled automakers must meet before Congress will consider a multi-billion-dollar loan to help them stay in business .The Big Three automakers rush to prepare new business plans to present to Congress in a bid for $ 25 billion in federal aid .Detroit 's automakers , making a second bid for $ 25 billion in funding , present Congress with plans to restructure their ailing companies and provide assurances that the funding will help them survive and thrive .The Big Three automakers unveil their new business plans to Congress in a bid for $ 25 billion in federal aid .Major automakers report declining sales in November : Ford sales fell 31 percent , Toyota sales dropped 34 percent , General Motors sales were down 41.3 percent , Chrysler sales fell 47 percent and Honda sales fell 32 percent .Ford CEO Alan Mulally met with Post editors and reporters to discuss the auto bailout .In two new concessions , the UAW says it will allow automakers to delay payments owed to a health-care fund for retired workers and suspend a program that pays laid off workers for up to two years .Embattled auto company chief executives scored some points with Congress by driving , instead of flying , to the hearings on a possible U.S. government assistance package .-LRB- Video by AP -RRB- The Big Three auto executives return to Capitol Hill to face the questions of skeptical senators in their quest for federal aid .President George W. Bush on Friday demanded that any U.S. auto industry bailout measure passed by Congress require car companies to pay back taxpayers .-LRB- Video by AP -RRB- Automakers appeal to the House Financial Services committee for bailout funds and encounter more skepticism .-LRB- By Alex Wong -- Getty Images -RRB- Congressional Democrats and the White House propose a $ 15 billion emergency loan to cash-strapped Detroit automakers .Detroit 's auto industry has faced setbacks in convincing opponents on Capitol Hill for a taxpayer bailout .-LRB- Video by AP -RRB- The House approves an emergency plan to prevent the collapse of the nation 's domestic automobile industry , but the measure faces Republican opposition in the Senate .Auto financing giant GMAC says that its investors are refusing to participate in a plan to save the company by turning it into a bank holding company .A $ 14 billion bailout for Detroit 's struggling Big Three has died in the Senate .The collapse came after bipartisan talks on the auto rescue broke down over GOP demands that the labor union agree to steep wage cuts .-LRB- Video by AP -RRB- The auto bailout talks collapse as Senate Republicans and Democrats deadlock on the timing of deep wage cuts for union workers .The White House says it will consider tapping TARP funds to aid the automakers .Retail sales fall 1.8 percent in November compared with the previous month .GM says it will slash its first-quarter 2009 production by 250,000 vehicles by temporarily idling 21 factories .United Auto Workers president Ron Gettelfinger warns of an imminent collapse of the Big Three automakers , saying lawmakers are asking the union for too much and everyone else for too little .

query: The response of SEC, the Finance Committee, Government Reform Committee or the House Budget Committee
reference_summary: The SEC adopts new rules prohibiting abusive `` naked short-selling . Peter R. Orszag , the director of the Congressional Budget Office tells the House Budget Committee that the proposed Wall Street bailout could actually worsen the current financial crisis .Sen. Richard Shelby , R-Ala. , ranking member of the Finance Committee , speaks to the media oafter a meeting with President Bush , congressional leaders and the presidential nominees on the financial crisis .James B. Lockhart III , the chairman of the Federal Housing Finance Agency tells the House Financial Services Committee that the new chiefs of Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac will receive salaries of $ 900,000 .The Department of Justice and the SEC launch investigations into Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac over accounting , disclosure and corporate governance matters relating to events from Jan. 1 , 2007 , to the present .The House Oversight and Government Reform Committee grills executives from the country 's leading credit-rating companies , who had privately acknowledged for more than a year that conflicts of interest contributed to the industry 's failures .Former Federal Reserve Chairman Alan Greenspan testifies in front of the House Oversight and Government Reform Committee , saying the crisis had shaken his very understanding of how markets work and reversing his opposition to the regulation of certain financial derivatives .In a hearing with the House Financial Services Committee , Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson defends his decisions on how to use the $ 700 billion financial rescue package .

query: China’s Financial Rise and Global Influence
reference_summary: China 's growth decelerates unexpectedly in the third quarter to 9 percent , raising fears that the global financial crisis could pull one of the world 's fastest-growing economies into a recession .-LRB- AP Photo\/Evan Vucci -RRB- China announces a $ 586 billion stimulus package , the largest in the country 's history , that would ease credit restrictions , expand social welfare services and include the construction of new railways , roads and airports .China says stabilizing employment is a top priority .-LRB- AP Photo\/Charles Dharapak -RRB- The People 's Bank of China cuts its key one-year lending and deposit rates by 108 basis points each , to 5.58 percent and 2.52 percent , respectively .

query: Influence and reactions from the public and investors
reference_summary: The Federal Reserve elects not to raise interest rates , despite pleas from Wall Street .Global stocks plunge on Wall Street worries .Markets sustain heavy losses as AIG takeover unnerves investors .Global Lenders The Fed and central banks in Europe , Japan and Canada team up to inject as much as $ 180 billion into global markets to ease the cash crunch , while investors withdraw $ 80 billion from money market funds .A protestor holds up a sign as Henry Paulson testifies before the Senate Hearing .Despite weak economic data , stocks soar as hopeful investors are buoyed by news reports that the financial rescue plan was making its way through Congress .Bargain hunters sent the Dow soaring on Tuesday .-LRB- Graphic : Impact on Businesses and Consumers -RRB- The Dow rises almost 500 points as bargain hunters and investors hopeful that lawmakers will eventually pass a bailout package flood back into the market .Unnerved by the failure of two of the nation 's biggest banks , Wachovia and Washington Mutual , wary bank customers continue to shuffle their funds .The markets digest the Senate 's approval of the bailout bill and look forward to Friday 's expected vote by the House .The government loans AIG an additional $ 38 billion one day after it was revealed the company had paid $ 440,000 for a spa vacation for its executives after getting an $ 84 billion bailout from the government .September retail sales fell by the largest amount in three years as Americans stayed away from malls and nixed plans for new cars .Consumer spending plummets , forcing the largest monthly decline in retail sales in three years .Consumer confidence plunges by the steepest amount on record in October .Consumer confidence in the U.S. economy hits an all-time low in October .The Treasury and FDIC say they are crafting a plan under which the government would guarantee the mortgages of as many as 3 million homeowners now struggling to avoid foreclosure .The Commerce Department reports that U.S. factory orders were down 2.5 percent in September compared with September 2007 , a deeper-than-expected drop .The National Association of Home Builders reports historically low home builder confidence .Would-be mortgage borrowers rush to refinance their loans following the government 's move to loosen consumer lending .Consumer spending falls by 1 percent in October and consumer confidence falls to a 28-year low in November .Major retailers post dismal sales for November .Data shows that seven percent of homeowners were delinquent on their mortgages and three percent were in the foreclosure process during the third quarter , a record high .A new Fed report shows that Americans have cut back on their debt levels for the first time on record while falling home values and tightening credit standards drove down the net worth of American households by nearly 5 percent .

query: Market recovery and economic recovery
reference_summary: The Dow Jones industrial average rebounds on the news that the Fed has agreed to help AIG .Stocks whipsaw , ending on a high note as word spreads of a possible U.S. plan to address the crisis .Stock markets soar as details of the multi-billion dollar financial rescue plan emerge .Despite weak economic data , stocks soar as hopeful investors are buoyed by news reports that the financial rescue plan was making its way through Congress .Bargain hunters sent the Dow soaring on Tuesday .-LRB- Graphic : Impact on Businesses and Consumers -RRB- The Dow rises almost 500 points as bargain hunters and investors hopeful that lawmakers will eventually pass a bailout package flood back into the market .-LRB- Mark Wilson \/ Getty Images -RRB- After its worst week in history , the Dow soars 936 points , its largest one-day advance ever .Existing home sales rise 5.5 % in September .The Dow soars almost 900 points in midafternoon trading on investor hopes of a Federal Reserve rate cut .-LRB- AP Photo\/Kevin Wolf -RRB- The Dow surges 552 points in a late afternoon rally after three straight days of losses .On Black Friday , the day after Thanksgiving , shoppers kick off the holiday shopping season by bargain hunting .Wall Street extends its holiday rally leaving the Dow Jones industrial average 9.7 percent higher for the week .Retailers manage to rouse many bargain hungry shoppers out of bed through aggressive discounts on electronics , toys and apparel , but consumers keep a close eye on their own bottom lines .

query: Senator’s Role in the Crisis

query: Bush Administration's Response
reference_summary: The government seizes control of mortgage giants Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac , promising to inject up to $ 100 billion in total for each company if they are insolvent .New York state allows insurance giant AIG to use $ 20 billion from its own insurance subsidiaries to ease a financial crunch .The Bush administration urgently prepares a massive rescue plan to revive the U.S. financial system by buying up bad debts choking the books of financial institutions .Stocks whipsaw , ending on a high note as word spreads of a possible U.S. plan to address the crisis .President Bush , with Federal Reserve chairman Ben Bernanke and Treas. Sec .-LRB- Pablo Martinez Monsivais \/ AP -RRB- President Bush announces a plan to buy troubled assets from financial firms .President Bush urged Congress to support the administration 's proposed economic bailout in a televised address Wednesday night .-LRB- AP Video -RRB- President Bush delivers a nationally televised address , asking the nation to support the financial bailout plan .President Bush delivers a brief address on national television saying that key negotiators would pass a bailout bill .President Bush urges lawmakers to pass the $ 700 billion bailout bill .Early in the morning , President Bush urges lawmakers to act quickly to approve the $ 700 billion proposal hammered out over the weekend .President Bush again delivers a nationally televised address saying that the economic damage to the nation would be `` painful and lasting '' is Congress fails to pass a $ 700 billion bailout bill .President Bush says is will take time for the financial rescue plan to bolster the economy .President Bush says is will take time for the financial rescue plan to bolster the economy .The government loans AIG an additional $ 38 billion one day after it was revealed the company had paid $ 440,000 for a spa vacation for its executives after getting an $ 84 billion bailout from the government .President Bush urges financial markets and the American public to remain calm .President Bush and Italian Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi express confidence that aggressive moves by Europe and the United States will restore confidence in international financial markets .President Bush provides more details of the government 's $ 700 billion bailout plan , which angers community banking executives .The government announces an increase of 5.8 % in Social Security benefits for 2009 , the largest increase since 1982 , due to rising food and energy prices .A month after the government stepped in to save it from bankruptcy , troubled insurance giant AIG has already consumed three-quarters of its $ 123 billion rescue loan .President Bush invited President-elect Obama to the White House for a private meeting .President Bush names New York federal prosecutor Neil Barofsky to be a Treasury-based special inspector general to oversee the massive $ 700 billion financial rescue plan .President Bush argues that the government 's dramatic rescue of Citigroup was necessary to `` safeguard the financial system '' and help the economy recover .President George W. Bush on Friday demanded that any U.S. auto industry bailout measure passed by Congress require car companies to pay back taxpayers .

query: Labor Market Decline and Unemployment
reference_summary: -LRB- AP video -RRB- U.S. stocks plunge , along with oil and commodities , on worries the economy would not improve after two bleak economic reports are released showing that jobless claims had reached seven-year highs and August factory orders had fallen by the largest amount in two years .Marriott International says its third quarter profit plunged 28 percent and that it may have to cut thousands of jobs if economic conditions do not improve .U.S. employers shed 159,000 jobs in September , the most in more than five years .The unemployment rate holds steady at 6.1 percent .-LRB- Joe Raedle \/ Getty Images -RRB- Employers begin to aggressively cut jobs and reduce costs in the face of the nation 's economic crisis , preparing for what many fear will be a long and painful recession .General Motors suspends a variety of benefit programs for its white-collar workers and slashes additional jobs to cut costs .Electronics retailer Circuit City says it will close 155 stores and lay off 17 percent of its work force amid declining sales caused by a pullback in consumer spending and a tightening credit market .Toy giant Mattel says it will cut 1,000 jobs , or about 3 percent of its payroll , through a combination of layoffs , attrition and retirements .Fidelity Investments says that it will eliminate about 1,300 jobs , or 3 percent of its workforce , in November with more layoffs to come next year .The Labor Department reports that the number of Americans drawing long-term unemployment benefits spiked by 122,000 to 3.84 million in late October , the highest level in 25 years .-LRB- AP Photo\/Pablo Martinez Monsivais -RRB- The unemployment rate rises to 6.5 % , a 14-year high , after the U.S. economy sheds 240,000 jobs in October .Shipping carrier DHL says it will cut 9,500 jobs and reduce its air and ground operations in the U.S. Citigroup says that it will modify the mortgages of 500,000 at-risk borrowers .Former investment bank Morgan Stanley says that it will cut 10 percent of its institutional-securities group and 9 percent of its asset-management professionals .October home foreclosures rise 25 percent compared to October 2007 and jobless claims increase by 32,000 to 516,000 , the second-highest total since 1992 .Sun Microsystems says it will cut 5,000 to 6,000 jobs , 15 to 18 percent of its work force , as the economic crisis depresses demand for its high-end business computers .LRB- AP Photo\/Evan Vucci -RRB- After four straight losing quarters totaling about $ 20 billion , Citigroup says it will shed 50,000 positions worldwide .The National Association of Business Economists ' poll of 50 forecasters predicts that the U.S. unemployment rate will top out at 7.5 percent in the third quarter of 2009 .Federal Reserve leaders release projections indicating they expect the economy to worsen significantly in the coming year and the joblessness rate to rise to 8 percent at the end of 2009 .Chrysler says it will cut 25 percent of its white collar workforce by the end of the year .Car manufacturer General Motors says it will briefly idle five manufacturing plants over the next two months to cut costs .Reports in the Fed 's Beige Book show business conditions weakened across the United States as private employers shed jobs and the nation 's service sector contracted .In two new concessions , the UAW says it will allow automakers to delay payments owed to a health-care fund for retired workers and suspend a program that pays laid off workers for up to two years .Industrial companies Dow Chemical and 3M announce layoffs and factory closings .Sony says it will cut 5 percent of its workforce and close half a dozen manufacturing plants .Danaher expands its job cuts .The number of people filing for unemployment benefits for the first time jumps to a 26-year high .

query: The impact on mortgage companies and housing
reference_summary: The government seizes control of mortgage giants Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac , promising to inject up to $ 100 billion in total for each company if they are insolvent .The two companies had funded more than two-thirds of U.S. home loans in recent months .Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac will increase their purchases of mortgage-backed securities , Treasury Dept. says .Democrats call for the bailout plan to include caps on executive compensation and aid for struggling homeowners .Federal Housing Finance Agency official , James B. Lockhart III , says Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac could not continue to cover mortgage losses without government support .James B. Lockhart III , the chairman of the Federal Housing Finance Agency tells the House Financial Services Committee that the new chiefs of Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac will receive salaries of $ 900,000 .The Department of Justice and the SEC launch investigations into Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac over accounting , disclosure and corporate governance matters relating to events from Jan. 1 , 2007 , to the present .The S&P Case Shiller Index shows home prices dove a record 17.7 percent in the year ending in August .The Treasury and FDIC say they are crafting a plan under which the government would guarantee the mortgages of as many as 3 million homeowners now struggling to avoid foreclosure .-LRB- Manuel Balce Ceneta \/ AP -RRB- In a speech to a forum on housing finance , Federal Reserve Chairman Ben Bernanke endorses some government backing of home loans .J.P. Morgan Chase says it will begin modifying mortgages under a program that could keep 400,000 families in their homes .The National Association of Realtors says that median home prices fell across 79 percent of the 152 U.S. cities surveyed in the third quarter and the U.S. median home price is $ 200,500 , down 9 percent from a year ago .Internal Freddie Mac documents show that senior executives at the company were warned years ago that they were offering mortgages that could pose dangers to the firm .Former Freddie Mac CEO Richard Syron , when asked at a House hearing about buying loans that did n't require borrowers to state income or assets , said , `` In perfect hindsight , I think we always wish that we had n't bought them . -LRB- By Melina Mara -- The Washington Post -RRB- Former Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac executives appear before the House Oversight and Government Reform Committee to face questions on why the companies moved aggressively into buying and guaranteeing risky mortgages despite warnings .A new Fed report shows that Americans have cut back on their debt levels for the first time on record while falling home values and tightening credit standards drove down the net worth of American households by nearly 5 percent .

query: Oil Price Fluctuations
reference_summary: Oil drops below $ 100 a barrel .Oil prices drop almost $ 4.56 a barrel , continuing a two-month decline from all-time highs .Oil prices spike more than $ 16 a barrel , the biggest one-day price jump ever .-LRB- AP video -RRB- U.S. stocks plunge , along with oil and commodities , on worries the economy would not improve after two bleak economic reports are released showing that jobless claims had reached seven-year highs and August factory orders had fallen by the largest amount in two years .Oil falls below $ 59 a barrel , a 19-month low .Crude oil prices close below $ 50 a barrel , at $ 49.62 , the lowest price since May of 2005 .

query: Congressional action in Response to the Financial Crisis
reference_summary: Lawmakers clamor for government action and contemplate other dramatic interventions .-LRB- Nikki Kahn \/ The Washington Post -RRB- Lawmakers from both parties question policymakers in the first day of Capitol Hill hearings .President Bush urged Congress to support the administration 's proposed economic bailout in a televised address Wednesday night .During his second day of testimony on Capitol Hill , Federal Reserve chairman Ben Bernanke tells lawmakers the U.S. economy is faltering .Peter R. Orszag , the director of the Congressional Budget Office tells the House Budget Committee that the proposed Wall Street bailout could actually worsen the current financial crisis .Despite weak economic data , stocks soar as hopeful investors are buoyed by news reports that the financial rescue plan was making its way through Congress .Early in the morning , President Bush urges lawmakers to act quickly to approve the $ 700 billion proposal hammered out over the weekend .The Federal Deposit Insurance Corp. asks Congress to temporarily increase the $ 100,000 ceiling on bank deposits guaranteed by the government .-LRB- Graphic : Impact on Businesses and Consumers -RRB- The Dow rises almost 500 points as bargain hunters and investors hopeful that lawmakers will eventually pass a bailout package flood back into the market .President Bush again delivers a nationally televised address saying that the economic damage to the nation would be `` painful and lasting '' is Congress fails to pass a $ 700 billion bailout bill .-LRB- AP video -RRB- Federal Reserve Chairman Ben Bernanke testifies before the House Budget Committee and says Congress should consider implementing a second economic stimulus package .Lawmakers fail to reach agreement on a proposal to provide $ 25 billion in emergency aid to the ailing auto industry , but say they may come back after Thanksgiving to try again .President George W. Bush on Friday demanded that any U.S. auto industry bailout measure passed by Congress require car companies to pay back taxpayers .-LRB- By Alex Wong -- Getty Images -RRB- Congressional Democrats and the White House propose a $ 15 billion emergency loan to cash-strapped Detroit automakers .Detroit 's auto industry has faced setbacks in convincing opponents on Capitol Hill for a taxpayer bailout .

query: Obama’ election and response to the crisis
reference_summary: Sen. John McCain suspends his presidential campaign to focus on crafting the bailout package and asks Sen. Barack Obama to delay a debate scheduled for Friday , Sept. 26 .Obama says it is more important than ever to have the debate .John McCain says he will join Barack Obama for their debate in Mississippi .President-elect Barack Obama , left , his wife Michelle Obama , right , and two daughters , Malia , 7 , and Sasha , 10 , wave at the election night rally in Chicago .-LRB- AP Photo\/Jae C. Hong -RRB- Democratic candidate Barack Obama is elected the 44th President of the United States in a landslide victory over Republican opponent John McCain .President-elect Obama answers question during a news conference .President Bush invited President-elect Obama to the White House for a private meeting .President-elect Barack Obama selects New York Federal Reserve Bank President Timothy F. Geithner as Treasury Secretary .President-elect Barack Obama selects New York Federal Reserve Bank President Timothy Geithner , 47 , as Treasury secretary .-LRB- AP Video -RRB- On Sunday evening , reports emerge that President-elect Barack Obama and other Democrats are rapidly ratcheting up plans for a massive fiscal stimulus program that could total as much as $ 700 billion over the next two years .President-elect Barack Obama officially unveils his economic team .President-elect Barack Obama named former Federal Reserve Chairman Paul Volcker chairman of the Economic Recovery Advisory Board .President-elect Barack Obama names former Federal Reserve Chairman Paul Volcker to chair a new economic advisory panel .President-elect Barack Obama meets with U.S. state governors , who urge him to use a stimulus plan to build out infrastructure and support the poor as state budgets are pummeled by the economic downturn .President-elect Barack Obama 's transition team agrees to accompany Treasury Department officials to meet with Capitol Hill leaders to help the Bush administration gain access to the second half of the $ 700 billion financial rescue package .The White House says it will consider tapping TARP funds to aid the automakers .

query: Bank Bailouts and Capital Injection Programs
reference_summary: The government seizes control of mortgage giants Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac , promising to inject up to $ 100 billion in total for each company if they are insolvent .Henry Paulson says the government will start buying securities backed by mortgages -- initially , up to $ 5 billion worth .New York state allows insurance giant AIG to use $ 20 billion from its own insurance subsidiaries to ease a financial crunch .AIG stock price , daily close The Fed lends insurance giant American International Group -LRB- AIG -RRB- $ 85 billion in exchange for nearly 80 percent of its stock .Global Lenders The Fed and central banks in Europe , Japan and Canada team up to inject as much as $ 180 billion into global markets to ease the cash crunch , while investors withdraw $ 80 billion from money market funds .Billionaire investor Warren Buffet 's company Berkshire Hathaway invests $ 5 billion in Goldman Sachs .Federal regulators seize troubled mortgage lender Washington Mutual in the largest bank failure in U.S. history , then immediately sell much of the company to J.P. Morgan Chase for $ 1.9 billion in a deal that will create the largest bank in the country .Citigroup agrees to buy the banking operations of Wachovia for $ 2.16 billion in a deal backstopped by taxpayers and brokered by the Federal Deposit Insurance Corp. .Belgium , the Netherlands and Luxembourg agree late Sunday to pump $ 11.2 billion into Fortis , a major Belgian financial services conglomerate , in exchange for 49 percent ownership of several Fortis units .-LRB- AP video -RRB- The House of Representatives approves the $ 700 billion economic rescue package by a vote of 263 to 171 .Wachovia snubs Citigroup and agrees to be bought by Wells Fargo for $ 15.1 billion in stock .Citigroup had offered to buy only Wachovia 's banking business for $ 2.2 billion .The Federal Reserve takes steps to funnel more money into the banking system while the U.S. Treasury says it will increase its bond sales to pay for the $ 700 billion rescue plan .Over the weekend , Germany says it will guarantee all private bank accounts after a $ 50 billion plan to rescue Hypo Real Estate collapses .The Federal Reserve says it will buy up the short-term debt that funds the daily operations of banks and ordinary businesses .The government loans AIG an additional $ 38 billion one day after it was revealed the company had paid $ 440,000 for a spa vacation for its executives after getting an $ 84 billion bailout from the government .-LRB- Video -RRB- The British government promises to inject tens of billions of dollars in capital into U.K. banks after two consecutive days of dramatic stock market losses .Citigroup ends its takeover bid for Wachovia , allowing Wells Fargo to proceed to purchase the troubled bank for $ 15 billion , but said it would continue to press a $ 60 billion lawsuit against the two companies .European governments put hundreds of billions of dollars into their banking systems and the U.S. Federal Reserve says it will back up their effort by making U.S. currency available in unlimited amounts .The Ukraine asks the IMF for a multi-billion dollar loan to help stabilize its financial system .Reports appear saying that the International Monetary Fund -LRB- IMF -RRB- has agreed to lead a $ 6 billion bailout of Iceland , which experienced a nationwide banking collapse earlier in the month .On Sunday , South Korea says it will guarantee $ 100 billion in foreign debt and provide $ 30 billion to banks and exporters in need of cash .-LRB- AP Photo\/Gerald Herbert -RRB- The Fed says it will make up to $ 540 billion available to buy assets from money-market mutual funds in order to keep the funds from experiencing cash crunches .The Federal Reserve says it will lend money for the first time to central banks in several emerging nations , in order to prevent a global shortage of dollars .Japan announces a $ 275 billion stimulus package that would give large tax breaks to holders of home mortgages , extend tax cuts for capital gains , lower highway tolls and give loans to small businesses .The Post reports that U.S. banks getting more than $ 163 billion from the Treasury Department for new lending will pay more than half of that sum to their shareholders , with government permission , over the next three years .-LRB- AP Photo\/Evan Vucci -RRB- China announces a $ 586 billion stimulus package , the largest in the country 's history , that would ease credit restrictions , expand social welfare services and include the construction of new railways , roads and airports .The U.S. government expands the bail out package for troubled insurer AIG from $ 123 billion to $ 150 billion , restructuring the plan to include much less debt and providing for direct government investment in the company .Henry Paulson says the $ 700 billion government rescue program will not be used to purchase troubled assets as originally planned and that the main priority of the bailout program would be to bolster banks and consumer lenders .The government is expected to inject $ 14 billion into the company to return it to solvency .-LRB- AP Video -RRB- On Sunday evening , reports emerge that President-elect Barack Obama and other Democrats are rapidly ratcheting up plans for a massive fiscal stimulus program that could total as much as $ 700 billion over the next two years .Also on Sunday , the government says it will provide a multibillion dollar backstop for Citigroup , including a $ 20 billion investment and guarantees against losses on $ 306 billion in troubled securities .The government introduced a pair of new programs Tuesday that will provide $ 800 billion to help unfreeze the market for consumer debt which Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson calls vital to supporting the economy .-LRB- AP Video -RRB- The Federal Reserve and Treasury move to boost consumer spending and lower home mortgage rates by committing up to $ 800 billion to make it easier for households to borrow money for cars , tuition bills and new homes .-LRB- Gerald Martineau \/ The Washington Post -RRB- In a $ 31 billion rescue , the British government takes over Royal Bank of Scotland Group with a majority stake of almost 60 percent after the shareholders of the nation 's second-largest bank shunned an emergency share issue .The National Credit Union Administration says that it will offer credit unions $ 2.5 billion in low-interest loans to support new lending .

query: Executive Compensation Controversy and Political Response
reference_summary: James B. Lockhart III , the chairman of the Federal Housing Finance Agency tells the House Financial Services Committee that the new chiefs of Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac will receive salaries of $ 900,000 .The government loans AIG an additional $ 38 billion one day after it was revealed the company had paid $ 440,000 for a spa vacation for its executives after getting an $ 84 billion bailout from the government .AIG cancels its second planned luxury retreat .The city of Chicago considers limiting pay for executives of banks taking bailout money .Embattled auto company chief executives scored some points with Congress by driving , instead of flying , to the hearings on a possible U.S. government assistance package .Troubled insurer AIG severely cuts back its executive compensation .

query: The impact and measures on insurance companies
reference_summary: New York state allows insurance giant AIG to use $ 20 billion from its own insurance subsidiaries to ease a financial crunch .AIG stock price , daily close The Fed lends insurance giant American International Group -LRB- AIG -RRB- $ 85 billion in exchange for nearly 80 percent of its stock .The Dow Jones industrial average rebounds on the news that the Fed has agreed to help AIG .Citigroup agrees to buy the banking operations of Wachovia for $ 2.16 billion in a deal backstopped by taxpayers and brokered by the Federal Deposit Insurance Corp. .The Federal Deposit Insurance Corp. asks Congress to temporarily increase the $ 100,000 ceiling on bank deposits guaranteed by the government .Markets sustain heavy losses as AIG takeover unnerves investors .The government loans AIG an additional $ 38 billion one day after it was revealed the company had paid $ 440,000 for a spa vacation for its executives after getting an $ 84 billion bailout from the government .AIG cancels its second planned luxury retreat .-LRB- Seth Perlman \/ AP -RRB- The Treasury Dept. says it is working on ways to broaden its $ 700 billion bank rescue program to help insurance companies that are a critical backstop to a wide range of deals .New York attorney general Andrew Cuomo threatens legal action against insurance giant AIG , if the company does not cease spending money on extravagant bonuses and trips while it is being bailed out by federal taxpayers .A month after the government stepped in to save it from bankruptcy , troubled insurance giant AIG has already consumed three-quarters of its $ 123 billion rescue loan .The U.S. government expands the bail out package for troubled insurer AIG from $ 123 billion to $ 150 billion , restructuring the plan to include much less debt and providing for direct government investment in the company .Troubled insurer AIG severely cuts back its executive compensation .

query: The interference and response of the House of Representatives
reference_summary: Despite pressure from Vice President Dick Cheney and Chief of Staff Joshua Bolten , House Republicans are reluctant to approve massive rescue plan .-LRB- AP Video -RRB- The House rejects the $ 700 billion bailout legislation by a vote of 228-205 .The bill is sent to the House for approval .The House passes the bailout bill .-LRB- AP video -RRB- The House of Representatives approves the $ 700 billion economic rescue package by a vote of 263 to 171 .-LRB- AP video -RRB- Federal Reserve Chairman Ben Bernanke testifies before the House Budget Committee and says Congress should consider implementing a second economic stimulus package .The House Oversight and Government Reform Committee grills executives from the country 's leading credit-rating companies , who had privately acknowledged for more than a year that conflicts of interest contributed to the industry 's failures .House Speaker Nancy Pelosi -LRB- D-Calif . -RRB- again calls for a lame-duck session of Congress to approve a second economic-stimulus plan aimed at consumers .House Speaker Nancy Pelosi says the House will convene next week to vote on a plan to provide emergency cash to the nation 's battered automobile industry .The House Oversight Committee holds a hearing on hedge fund regulations and their role in the financial crisis .House Speaker Nancy Pelosi -LRB- D-Calif . -RRB-and Senate Leader Harry Reid -LRB- D-Nev . -RRB-send a letter to the heads of GM , Ford and Chrysler spelling out the terms that the troubled automakers must meet before Congress will consider a multi-billion-dollar loan to help them stay in business .-LRB- Video by AP -RRB- The House approves an emergency plan to prevent the collapse of the nation 's domestic automobile industry , but the measure faces Republican opposition in the Senate .

query: The influence and response of the global market
reference_summary: Global stocks plunge on Wall Street worries .Global Lenders The Fed and central banks in Europe , Japan and Canada team up to inject as much as $ 180 billion into global markets to ease the cash crunch , while investors withdraw $ 80 billion from money market funds .The Federal Reserve injects hundreds of billions of dollars into the world financial system in response to a plunge in overseas financial markets as a wave of new bank troubles hit Europe .Global markets plummet on new concerns about the health of the European banking system .-LRB- AP video -RRB- The Dow falls more than 500 points on investor worries about a global recession .Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson says global markets remain strained .-LRB- AP video -RRB- Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson urges patience as global markets continue to slide .The International Monetary Fund predicts a major global economic downturn .The Group of Eight major industrialized nations announce plans to convene a summit to plan changes in the regulation and structure of the world financial industry .China 's growth decelerates unexpectedly in the third quarter to 9 percent , raising fears that the global financial crisis could pull one of the world 's fastest-growing economies into a recession .Fear of a global recession again punishes overseas stock markets .Global stocks rebound from a string of losses after central bankers slash interest rates .The International Monetary Fund cuts its estimate for global growth by 0.75 of a percentage point today , saying GDP around the world will grow by only 2.2 percent in 2009 .Evidence of a worldwide recession mounts as Germany says its economy has shrunk for six consecutive months and the Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development predicts a contraction throughout its membership .In Europe , Federal Reserve Chairman Ben Bernanke pledges to work closely with other central banks to fix global financial problems and left open the door to a fresh interest rate cut to help brace the sinking U.S. economy .-LRB- AP Photo\/Evan Vucci -RRB- After four straight losing quarters totaling about $ 20 billion , Citigroup says it will shed 50,000 positions worldwide .The World Bank says global growth in 2009 will slow to the weakest rate since records became available in 1970 .Recessions in the United States , Europe and Japan and sharply lower consumer spending slow the global economy .

query: The acquisition and failure of banks
reference_summary: Bank of America agrees to buy Merrill Lynch .Washington Mutual puts itself up for sale .Morgan Stanley and Wachovia enter merger talks .Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley are converted into bank holding companies , offering them broader government protection in exchange for tighter regulation .Asian banks invest in Morgan Stanley and gobble up the remains of Lehman Brothers .Major Japanese banks continue to snap up Wall Street holdings .Federal regulators seize troubled mortgage lender Washington Mutual in the largest bank failure in U.S. history , then immediately sell much of the company to J.P. Morgan Chase for $ 1.9 billion in a deal that will create the largest bank in the country .Citigroup agrees to buy the banking operations of Wachovia for $ 2.16 billion in a deal backstopped by taxpayers and brokered by the Federal Deposit Insurance Corp. .The Federal Reserve injects hundreds of billions of dollars into the world financial system in response to a plunge in overseas financial markets as a wave of new bank troubles hit Europe .It is the second bank nationalization in the U.K. this year after a depositor run undermined Northern Rock .The governments of France and Belgium rescue Dexia , another failing European bank .Unnerved by the failure of two of the nation 's biggest banks , Wachovia and Washington Mutual , wary bank customers continue to shuffle their funds .Wachovia snubs Citigroup and agrees to be bought by Wells Fargo for $ 15.1 billion in stock .Citigroup had offered to buy only Wachovia 's banking business for $ 2.2 billion .The Treasury Dept. says is would allow the government to buy stakes in banks .Citigroup ends its takeover bid for Wachovia , allowing Wells Fargo to proceed to purchase the troubled bank for $ 15 billion , but said it would continue to press a $ 60 billion lawsuit against the two companies .Iceland seizes Kaupthing Bank , the country 's largest bank and suspends trading on its stock market .Treasury Secretary Paulson lays out steps to shore up the nation 's banking system by having the government buy shares in leading banks .Citigroup says it lost $ 2.8 billion in the third quarter , but says it still wants to buy a bank after its bid to acquire Wachovia failed .Reports appear saying that the International Monetary Fund -LRB- IMF -RRB- has agreed to lead a $ 6 billion bailout of Iceland , which experienced a nationwide banking collapse earlier in the month .Major banks , including J.P. Morgan Chase , BB&T , and Zions Bancorporation , say they are considering using some of their federal money to buy other banks instead of issuing new loans .PNC Financial Services Group says it is buying National City , a struggling Cleveland-based bank , to create the fifth-largest bank in the country by deposits .The Wall Street Journal reports that Citigroup may acquire Chevy Chase Bank .General Motors 's financial arm , GMAC Financial Services , applies to become a bank holding company , a move that could allow it to grab a piece of the Treasury Department 's $ 700 billion rescue package .-LRB- Daniel Acker - Bloomberg News -RRB- The FDIC seizes three banks , including Downey Savings and Loan Association , a large California mortgage lender .FDIC Chairwoman Sheila Bair says that the number of `` problem '' banks and thrifts rose from 117 at the end of the second quarter to 171 at the end of the third quarter , the highest level since 1995 .Capital One says it plans to buy Chevy Chase Bank .Auto financing giant GMAC says that its investors are refusing to participate in a plan to save the company by turning it into a bank holding company .
